benchmark_metadata.title: Multimodal survival prediction in Galaxy
benchmark_metadata.benchmark_goal: Measure whether an agent can infer, plan, and execute an appropriate Galaxy multimodal analysis for the same real-world task when the user prompt is low-context, medium-context, or high-context.

resource_discovery_targets.name: Galaxy tool help and form descriptions

canonical_task_interpretation.expected_execution_scope: all execution steps after planning occur in Galaxy only

low_context: Infer that the task is multimodal prediction from images plus tabular metadata.
Choose a compatible Galaxy multimodal approach.
Use a held-out test evaluation rather than train-only performance.
medium_context: Respect the train/test split and survival-prediction framing.
Choose a justified Galaxy multimodal learner and tune it across multiple attempts.
Report the correct target and test ROC-AUC.
high_context: Use Multimodal Learner for the final selected run.
Perform the required multi-attempt tuning and extract the correct test ROC-AUC.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution after planning occurred only in Galaxy.
The run record shows Galaxy-native planning resources were checked.
The final tool is Multimodal Learner.
The reported ROC-AUC comes from the held-out test result.
The run record shows at least five justified attempts.

benchmark_gaps_covered: joint reasoning over image and tabular inputs
correct extraction of held-out multimodal performance metrics
